items.title: Moody’s Flags Rising Risks in Indonesia on Policy Uncertainty
items.description: Moody’s Ratings remains concerned about policy uncertainty and fiscal sustainability risks in Indonesia, reinforcing its cautious outlook and warning that downside risks are likely to persist.

items.title: Korean Stock Traders Slash Margin Loans to Lowest Since April
items.description: South Korean investors slashed their leveraged stock positions to the lowest level in three months, as memory-chip losses halted the market’s rally.

items.title: Global Stocks Lift Australian Pension Funds to 9.5% Annual Gain
items.description: A rally in global equities delivered another strong year for Australia’s A$4.4 trillion ($3.1 trillion) pension fund industry, despite lingering concerns over the Middle East conflict and stubborn inf

items.title: Australia’s Stock Market Emerges as Haven From Volatile AI Trade
items.description: Australia’s stock market has emerged as an unlikely haven from the artificial intelligence-driven volatility that has roiled Asian equities in recent months.

items.title: Gold Steady as Traders Watch Middle East War for Inflation Clues
items.description: Gold was steady as traders monitored a raft of developments in the Middle East conflict for clues on the energy price impact on inflation.

items.title: New Zealand’s Faster Inflation Bolsters Case for More Rate Hikes
items.description: New Zealand’s annual inflation accelerated to the fastest pace in more than two years, fanned by soaring fuel prices and bolstering the case for the Reserve Bank to execute back-to-back interest-rate

items.title: Kioxia Bulls Ride Out Volatility With Targets 130% Above Market
items.description: Analysts are maintaining bullish views on Kioxia Holdings Inc. despite a deepening share price correction, saying the stock is likely to resume its upward trajectory once supply-demand conditions impr

items.title: LA’s $1.2 Billion ‘Graffiti Towers’ Cleared to Exit Bankruptcy
items.description: The bankrupt $1.2 billion Oceanwide Plaza project in Los Angeles, dubbed the Graffiti Towers after taggers spray-painted the abandoned complex, has been cleared for sale and possible completion more t

items.title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 21
items.description: Oil steadied after a two-day gain as the US and Iran continued hostilities in the Middle East, while Yemen’s Houthi militants threatened to blockade Saudi Arabia’s maritime traffic in the Red Sea.

items.title: Asian Stocks Recover as Chips Rebound, Oil Dips: Markets Wrap
items.description: Asian equities advanced after three days of losses as a selloff in chip stocks eased ahead of megacap tech earnings this week.

items.title: DC Readies $1.2 Billion Bond Sale as Credit Stabilizes Post-DOGE
items.description: Washington, DC is stepping up to borrow $1.2 billion this week at its annual municipal debt sale, tapping the market as its credit stabilizes.

items.title: DoubleLine Says Higher Bond Yields to Help Fed Keep Rates Steady
items.description: DoubleLine is positioning in shorter-dated government bonds on the view that Kevin Warsh’s credibility with investors will help allow Federal Reserve officials to keep interest rates steady this year.

items.title: Gilts Rattled on Andy Burnham’s First Day as Fiscal Fears Return
items.description: UK government bonds dropped after new Prime Minister Andy Burnham unnerved investors on his first day in the job over his approach to the country’s finances.

items.title: Netflix Taps US High-Grade Bond Market With $1 Billion Debt Sale
items.description: Netflix Inc. raised $1 billion from a US high-grade bond sale on Monday, its first since a debut deal two years ago, as slowing sales growth fuels investor scrutiny of the company’s future.

items.title: S&P Slips as Apple, Tesla Keep Broader Chipmaker Gains in Check
items.description: The S&P 500 Index erased early gains to finish lower as a decline in Apple Inc. and Tesla Inc. offset advances elsewhere. Chipmakers rebounded ahead of a deluge of earnings reports that will test whet

items.title: Canada Financials Stocks Close In on Top Spot as Energy Momentum Fades
items.description: Canada’s financial stocks took a breather Monday after a torrid four-month rally that put them on the verge of overtaking energy as the best performing sector in the equity market this year.

items.title: Goldman Sells $2.5 Billion Preferreds Following Spread Low
items.description: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. priced new preferred shares Monday, just days after a metric determining risk and value in these securities hit its lowest level since the global financial crisis.

items.title: Latin America Outperforms as Emerging Market Currencies Gain
items.description: Emerging-market currencies gained to start the week, with Latin American names getting a boost from a drop in volatility which further boosts their carry appeal.

items.title: Stocks, Bonds Fall as US-Iran Risks Whipsaw Oil: Markets Wrap
items.description: A renewed bout of oil volatility sent stocks lower, with concerns about an escalation in Middle East hostilities outweighing attempts to bring about a pause in the war.

items.title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 20
items.description: Oil edged up in a fraught session as traders grappled with a barrage of headlines that suggest a worsening of the Middle East conflict, as well as attempts to bring about a pause in the war.
